title: Dryopteris morlae
intro: Dryopteris morlae, also known as Spanish shield fern, is a species of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique, leathery fronds with a distinctive shield-like shape. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris morlae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
taxonomy: Dryopteris morlae is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of wood fern native to the Iberian Peninsula. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or lady ferns. These ferns are characterized by their lance-shaped fronds and are often found in woodland environments. The classification of Dryopteris morlae places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida.
